    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Watch | India can become global pharma innovation hub with policy reforms: Lilly’s Patrik Jonsson]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/india-pharma-sector-needs-innovation-friendly-policies-lilly-international-19872709.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[India can emerge as a global hub for pharmaceutical innovation if it strengthens policy frameworks and aligns with global standards, Patrik Jonsson, Executive VP and President at Eli Lilly International, told CNBC-TV18. He said India has strong scientific talent and capabilities, but policy support will be key to unlocking its full potential.“India is very well positioned, with excellent scientists and strengths in drug discovery and development. However, there are opportunities to step up,” Jonsson said, calling for more predictable and innovation-friendly regulations.He highlighted that stronger intellectual property protection and regulatory data protection are critical. “Compulsory licensing is always a concern… a major opportunity for India is to implement regulatory data protection,” he noted, adding that such measures would signal that the country values innovation and attract more global investment.Jonsson’s comments come as Lilly deepens its India presence with a $1 billion manufacturing investment and expanded operations in Hyderabad and Bengaluru. The company is also betting on AI-led drug discovery through its partnership with Nvidia.While India remains a promising market, challenges around regulation, pricing and competition from generics persist, even as companies like Lilly push innovation in areas such as diabetes and obesity treatment.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Fri, 20 Mar 2026 21:04:37 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/india-pharma-sector-needs-innovation-friendly-policies-lilly-international-19872709.htm]]></guid>
      <category><![CDATA[Business]]></category>
      <media:content height="675" width="1200" medium="image" url="https://images.cnbctv18.com/uploads/2026/03/1774020879_KybKLDeY2d-eli-lilly-20marjpg-trUVh4B5Jk.jpg"/>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Watch | Luxor to enter creative segment with Crayola, bets on India’s young demographic: Pooja Gupta]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/luxor-crayola-partnership-india-strategy-pooja-gupta-19871983.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Luxor Writing Instruments is entering the creative segment through a partnership with Crayola, betting on India’s young demographic and rising discretionary spend, Managing Director Pooja Jain Gupta told CNBC-TV18.Calling it a “big change”, Gupta said the company is moving beyond pens into “the world of imagination”. She added, “Just as Luxor rewrote the history of writing with Parker 30 years ago, we are now set to rewrite this stage with colours and imagination through Crayola.”The bet is underpinned by strong market growth. The creativity and colouring segment is expanding at 15–20% annually and is projected to reach about ₹2,700 crore by 2030 from roughly ₹1,200 crore at present. “This brand is not just a utility product—it sells imagination as a product,” Gupta said.Luxor is also leveraging its nationwide distribution to scale the offering, while eyeing a larger play across manufacturing and exports. However, she flagged near-term supply challenges due to West Asia tensions, noting “shipments are delayed” though there are no significant freight cost increases yet.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Thu, 19 Mar 2026 20:43:37 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/luxor-crayola-partnership-india-strategy-pooja-gupta-19871983.htm]]></guid>
      <category><![CDATA[Business]]></category>
      <media:content height="675" width="1200" medium="image" url="https://images.cnbctv18.com/uploads/2026/03/1773933219_pyUiT4mrbM-luxor-19marjpg-dsCrx0hZmK.jpg"/>
    </item>
